event_overview.trigger: The status of a canonical record changes — either directly by a Data Owner or Source Processing, or as a result of a top-down status cascade from a parent record. One event is emitted per affected record including cascade-triggered descendants.
event_overview.business_description: Signals to downstream systems that the operational status of a canonical instrument has changed. Consumers gate their processing behaviour on canonical status — Active enables full data loading, Inactive enables reference data only, Disable suppresses all loading. The new_status field is included in the payload to allow consumers to act without a round-trip to the Query API.

registered_consumers.purpose: Gate data loading behaviour by canonical status — Active allows full ingestion, Inactive reference data only, Disable suppresses all loading
Refresh export eligibility — Active and Inactive records included per caller permissions, Disabled records excluded

fields.description: The canonical key hash of the record whose status changed
The new status value — one of: Active, Inactive, Disable. Included in payload to allow consumers to act without a round-trip to the Query API.
Identifies the change trigger — one of: User, SourceProcessing, Cascade. Cascade indicates the status change was propagated from a parent record.

producer_mapping.transform: Direct copy of new status value
Map to User | SourceProcessing | Cascade based on change actor

mappings.consumer_action: Identify which instrument's ingestion pipeline to adjust
Active → enable full ingestion; Inactive → reference data only; Disable → suppress all loading
Identify which instrument's export eligibility to refresh
Active/Inactive → include per caller permissions; Disable → exclude from all exports

schema_evolution.compatibility_strategy: Backward compatible — new optional fields may be added without a version bump; no existing fields (canonical_key, new_status, triggered_by) may be removed or have their type changed without a major version increment
schema_evolution.breaking_changes: Topic rename, field removal, field type change, new required field, partition key change — all require major version bump and 30-day consumer migration notice
schema_evolution.deprecation_policy: Deprecated fields must be flagged in the schema with a deprecation notice and remain present for a minimum of 30 days before removal
schema_evolution.versioning: Breaking changes result in a new event type (e.g., canonical.status.changed.v2) published in parallel until all consumers have migrated

delivery_sla.guarantee: At-least-once — consumers must be idempotent on (canonical_key, new_status) combination
delivery_sla.ordering: Ordered per canonical_key within a partition — status changes for the same record are processed in order
delivery_sla.latency: TBD — within platform event pipeline SLA; cascade events may produce a burst of messages for large hierarchies
delivery_sla.retry_policy: Standard Kafka producer retry — platform standard
delivery_sla.dead_letter_topic: nmd.canonical.status.changed.dlt — failed consumer messages routed here after max retries
delivery_sla.idempotency: Consumers must deduplicate on (canonical_key, event id); cascade scenarios may produce high event volumes — consumers should be designed for burst throughput

security.authentication: Kafka ACL — mTLS or SASL (TBD — platform standard)

authorization.consume: Data Ingestion Module (FRD-NMD-0002), Query & Export Module (FRD-NMD-0003)

security.pii_handling: No PII in payload — canonical_key is a deterministic hash; new_status and triggered_by are operational metadata only
security.compliance: Internal classification — no regulatory data handling requirements for this event

metrics: Event production rate (events/sec) — monitor for cascade bursts
Cascade event volume per parent change (to detect runaway cascades)
alerting: Alert on consumer lag exceeding threshold (TBD)
Alert on cascade event burst exceeding expected volume threshold (TBD)
observability.tracing: Propagate trace ID in CloudEvents extension header; link cascade child events to parent event trace for full cascade visibility
observability.logging: Producer logs canonical_key, new_status, triggered_by, and event ID on each publish; consumer logs canonical_key and new_status on receipt

entries.author: Leo
entries.description: Initial draft — status change notification carrying canonical_key, new_status, and triggered_by; one event per affected record including cascade-triggered descendants
